Title: TmCOP1-TmHY5 module-mediated blue light signal promotes chicoric acid biosynthesis in Taraxacum mongolicum

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, supplying entry flux into phenylpropanoid/chicoric acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream control step suggested, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active polyphenol with reported medicinal activities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chicoric acid precursor formation and contributes to increased chicoric acid accumulation when transcriptionally activated by TmHY5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; TmHY5 overexpression and CRISPR comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in TmHY5-OE2 than CR#9; blue-light-responsive pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in dual-LUC reporter; 35S promoter used for TmHY5 effector in tobacco transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmPAL3 was one of the chicoric acid biosynthetic genes significantly upregulated in TmHY5-OE2 versus CR#9. 2. TmHY5 activated the TmPAL3 promoter in dual-LUC assays. 3. TmHY5 directly bound G-box and AREB-like motifs in the TmPAL3 promoter by Y1H and EMSA, linking blue-light signaling to chicoric acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; white light; red light; ABA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among several PAL genes examined, TmPAL3 was the directly activated core target emphasized by functional assays.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway gene for boosting chicoric acid through promoter activation or stacking with TmHY5/TmbZIP1 in controlled light production systems.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxycinnamic acids such as p-coumaric acid/caffeic acid derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to CoA esters, providing acyl donors for downstream chicoric acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step strongly supported; central convergence point for TmHY5 and TmbZIP1 regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active polyphenol with medicinal value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chicoric acid accumulation; expression is directly activated by TmHY5 and indirectly enhanced through TmbZIP1, while TmCOP1 inhibits the TmHY5-Tm4CL1 module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; transcriptomics in OE/CR lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Blue-light-associated; significantly higher in TmHY5-OE2 than CR#9
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in dual-LUC reporter; 35S promoter used for TmHY5 and TmbZIP1 effectors in tobacco transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TmHY5; TmbZIP1; TmCOP1 (functional inhibition of TmHY5-Tm4CL1 module)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tm4CL1 was highly correlated with chicoric acid and was strongly induced in TmHY5-overexpressing lines. 2. TmHY5 directly activated the Tm4CL1 promoter via G-box/AREB-like motifs. 3. TmbZIP1 also promotes Tm4CL1, and TmHY5 plus TmbZIP1 act synergistically. 4. TmCOP1 inhibits the TmHY5-Tm4CL1 module, identifying Tm4CL1 as a core output node of blue-light signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; red light; white light; ABA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is the clearest mechanistic node connecting blue light, ABA signaling, TmHY5, TmbZIP1, and TmCOP1 effects on chicoric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority engineering target for chicoric acid enhancement; suitable for promoter engineering or stacking with TmHY5 and ABA-responsive regulators under blue-light cultivation.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA and quinate/tartaric-acid-related acyl acceptor pool
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxycinnamoyl esters linked to chicoric acid precursor metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from CoA esters to quinate-related acceptors and is implicated in chicoric acid/precursor biosynthesis in T. mongolicum.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxycinnamate ester formation branch linked to chicoric acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active polyphenol with medicinal value
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chicoric acid accumulation when activated by TmHY5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; TmHY5 OE/CR transcriptome comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in TmHY5-OE2 than CR#9; associated with blue-light-promoted chicoric acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in dual-LUC reporter; 35S promoter used for TmHY5 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmHQT2 expression was significantly higher in TmHY5-OE2 than CR#9. 2. TmHY5 activated the TmHQT2 promoter in dual-LUC assays. 3. TmHY5 directly bound G-box and AREB-like motifs in the TmHQT2 promoter by Y1H and EMSA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although HQT classically forms chlorogenic acid-related esters, this paper places TmHQT2 as a direct TmHY5 target contributing to chicoric acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target with TmHY5 for enhancing chicoric acid-related hydroxycinnamate esters.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, initiating flavonoid biosynthesis leading to luteolin and anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early flavonoid entry step feeding luteolin and anthocyanin branches
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control step, though not explicitly designated rate-limiting in paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive flavonoid/anthocyanin compounds involved in pigmentation and antioxidant properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances luteolin and anthocyanin accumulation when activated by TmHY5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; TmHY5 OE/CR transcriptome comparison
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in dual-LUC reporter; 35S promoter used for TmHY5 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmCHS2 was significantly induced in TmHY5-OE2 relative to CR#9. 2. TmHY5 enhanced TmCHS2 promoter activity in transient assays. 3. TmHY5 directly bound G-box and AREB-like motifs in the TmCHS2 promoter, explaining increased luteolin and anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TmHY5-driven luteolin increase was significant, whereas rutin was unchanged, indicating branch-specific downstream consequences.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with TmHY5 to enhance luteolin and anthocyanin pigmentation in controlled-environment cultivation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation when directly activated by TmHY5, contributing to red leaf stalk coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; transgenic metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in dual-LUC reporter; 35S promoter used for TmHY5 effector
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmANS2 expression was significantly elevated in TmHY5-OE2 and reduced in CR#9. 2. TmHY5 activated the TmANS2 promoter in dual-LUC assays. 3. TmHY5 directly bound G-box and AREB-like motifs in the TmANS2 promoter by Y1H and EMSA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This target explains the strong anthocyanin increase and red stalk phenotype in TmHY5-overexpressing lines.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering anthocyanin enrichment and visible pigmentation together with TmHY5.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; positive autoregulation; feedforward activation of downstream regulator; light-responsive control
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates TmPAL3, Tm4CL1, TmHQT2, TmCHS2, TmANS2, TmCOP1, TmHY5 and TmbZIP1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TmPAL3; Tm4CL1; TmHQT2; TmCHS2; TmANS2; TmCOP1; TmHY5; TmbZIP1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and branch activation of chicoric acid, luteolin and anthocyanins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal polyphenol accumulation, pigmentation, antioxidant-associated functions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive master regulator promoting chicoric acid, luteolin and anthocyanin accumulation under blue light.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key blue-light-responsive activator and central regulator linking light signaling to polyphenol biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blue light; ABA; NaCl; GA (transient repression then recovery); dark/light transitions
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation on its own promoter; activates TmCOP1 as feedback; activates TmbZIP1 feedforward branch to Tm4CL1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms inhibitory protein complex with TmCOP1; TmHY5-TmbZIP1 synergistically promotes Tm4CL1 expression functionally
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by blue light; highly expressed in flowers; transiently induced by ABA and NaCl; increased in OE lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in flowers; blue-light inducible
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression/transient assays; native promoter analyzed in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; CRISPR-Cas9 editing; metabolite profiling; transcriptomics; subcellular localization; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; pull-down; western blot
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmHY5 is induced by blue light and acts as the central positive regulator of chicoric acid biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increased chicoric acid, luteolin and anthocyanins, whereas CRISPR editing reduced them and impaired growth. 3. TmHY5 directly binds and activates TmPAL3, Tm4CL1, TmHQT2, TmCHS2 and TmANS2. 4. TmHY5 also activates TmbZIP1 and TmCOP1 and positively regulates itself, forming feedforward and feedback circuitry.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; white light; red light; ABA; GA; MeJA; NaCl; dark
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CRISPR loss-of-function reduced plant growth; pathway perturbation alters multiple polyphenol branches
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top candidate regulator for increasing chicoric acid in T. mongolicum using overexpression, promoter editing, or blue-light supplementation; should be balanced against growth effects and coupled branch changes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds multiple G-box and ACGT-containing motifs; discussion suggests possible context-dependent partnerships with BBX proteins though not directly validated here.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RING-type E3 ligase with RING, coiled-coil and WD40-repeat domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression; proteasome-linked inhibition of HY5 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly inhibits the TmHY5-Tm4CL1 module; negatively regulates polyphenol biosynthesis through interaction with TmHY5
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TmHY5 protein;complex activity on Tm4CL1 output
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Negative control of chicoric acid, luteolin and anthocyanin biosynthesis through light signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator; overexpression decreases chicoric acid, precursor phenolic acids, luteolin and anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent suppressor of blue-light-induced chicoric acid biosynthesis via the TmHY5 module
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blue light; white light; dark; ABA; proteasome inhibition by MG132
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Transcriptionally activated by TmHY5, forming feedback regulation; protein abundance reduced by light
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms TmCOP1-TmHY5 inhibitory complex; binding requires TmHY5 VP motif and TmCOP1 WD40 domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression induced by blue light and ABA within 2 h; protein is degraded more under blue/white light than dark
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in overexpression lines; native promoter in dual-LUC reporter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; metabolite profiling; Y2H; LCI; BiFC; pull-down; dual-LUC; Y1H; EMSA; western blot with light shift and MG132
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmCOP1 physically interacts with TmHY5 through its WD40 domain and the TmHY5 VP motif. 2. TmCOP1 overexpression reduces chicoric acid and related polyphenols. 3. Blue and white light weaken the TmCOP1-TmHY5 complex and promote TmCOP1 protein degradation, especially under blue light. 4. TmCOP1 suppresses the TmHY5-Tm4CL1 module but not the TmbZIP1-Tm4CL1 module.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; white light; red light; dark; ABA; MG132
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising knockout/downregulation target to enhance chicoric acid under controlled light cultivation; caution that COP1 broadly affects photomorphogenesis and light responses.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper interprets the response mainly through 26S ubiquitin-proteasome-linked repression of HY5 complex function under darkness.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of Tm4CL1; ABA-linked regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Tm4CL1; expression is directly activated by TmHY5
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indirect regulation of chicoric acid biosynthesis via ABA-responsive transcriptional module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive indirect regulator of chicoric acid biosynthesis downstream of TmHY5.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: ABA-responsive helper regulator that boosts Tm4CL1 and synergizes with TmHY5
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Acts in TmHY5-mediated feedforward branch to Tm4CL1; does not regulate TmHY5 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional synergy with TmHY5 on Tm4CL1 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in TmHY5-OE2; similar ABA-responsive expression pattern to TmHY5
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA; 35S promoter used in prior functional context and dual-LUC effectors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome comparison; Y1H; EMSA; dual-LUC synergy assay; prior study referenced in paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TmbZIP1 is upregulated in TmHY5-overexpressing lines. 2. TmHY5 directly binds ABRE motifs in the TmbZIP1 promoter. 3. TmHY5 and TmbZIP1 synergistically activate Tm4CL1, providing an indirect route by which TmHY5 promotes chicoric acid biosynthesis. 4. TmCOP1 does not significantly inhibit the TmbZIP1-Tm4CL1 module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with TmHY5 or used in ABA/light-integrated engineering to strengthen Tm4CL1 and chicoric acid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its direct role on Tm4CL1 was established previously and leveraged here as part of the TmHY5 feedforward module.
